articles.title: NASA Wants to Put Nuclear Reactors on the Moon
articles.description: The White House has announced that NASA will work with the Departments of Defense and Energy to put nuclear reactors in orbit and on the surface of the moon.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: Where the DOGE Operatives Are Now
articles.description: WIRED tracked down some of the most prominent figures of last year’s DOGE invasion. Here's where they are now—in government and beyond.
articles.author: By Vittoria Elliott

articles.title: New Gas-Powered Data Centers Could Emit More Greenhouse Gases Than Entire Nations
articles.description: A WIRED review of permits for data center projects using natural gas and linked to OpenAI, Meta, Microsoft, and xAI shows they could emit more than 129 million tons of greenhouse gases per year.
articles.author: By Molly Taft

articles.title: A Billionaire-Backed Startup Wants to Grow 'Organ Sacks' to Replace Animal Testing
articles.description: R3 Bio has a bold idea for replacing lab animals: genetically-engineered whole organ systems that lack a brain. The long-term goal, says a cofounder, is to make human versions.
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: How One Syrian Fought to the Death for a Free Internet
articles.description: Basel Khartabil hoped the internet would lead to a flowering of freedom and openness in Syria. Then he was arrested and imprisoned by the Assad regime.
articles.author: By Alice Su

articles.title: Airfare Keeps Going Up. Here Are Some Tricks to Finding Cheap(er) Tickets
articles.description: It's an expensive time to fly. These tips can help lighten the load on your wallet.
articles.author: By Aarian Marshall

articles.title: Opposing ICE Might Save the Country. It Could Also Ruin Your Life
articles.description: For months, lone vibe coder Rafael Concepcion has obsessively built tools to counter the federal immigration crackdown—pivoting as he’s been outmatched. He’s also lost his job and become a target.
articles.author: By Brendan I. Koerner

articles.title: A New Implant Aims to Rewire the Brain to Help Stroke Patients
articles.description: Epia Neuro’s brain-computer interface will include a motorized glove to help stroke patients recover movement in their hand.
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: Neuroscientists Decipher Procrastination: A Brain Mechanism Explains Why People Leave Certain Tasks for Later
articles.description: New research has discovered that a neural circuit may explain procrastination. Scientists were able to disrupt this connection using a drug.
articles.author: By Fernanda González

articles.title: This Scammer Used an AI-Generated MAGA Girl to Grift ‘Super Dumb’ Men
articles.description: A med student says he’s made thousands of dollars selling photos and videos of a young conservative woman he created using generative tools. He’s not alone.
articles.author: By Ej Dickson

articles.title: The 24 Best Movies on Amazon Prime Right Now
articles.description: “Sinners,” “Wicked,” and “Die Hard” are just a few of the movies you should be watching on Amazon Prime Video this week.
articles.author: By Matt Kamen

articles.title: US Special Forces Soldier Arrested for Polymarket Bets on Maduro Raid
articles.description: The master sergeant allegedly used classified intel to profit on the capture of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro, marking the first US arrest for insider trading on a prediction market.
articles.author: By Kate Knibbs

articles.title: China Approves the First Brain Chips for Sale—and Has a Plan to Dominate the Industry
articles.description: While the United States and Europe are moving cautiously forward with clinical trials, China is racing toward the commercialization of brain implants.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: These New Smart Glasses From Ex-OnePlus Engineers Have a Hidden Cost
articles.description: The Kickstarter-funded glasses from L'Atitude 52°N have AI features bundled for one year, but the company doesn't know yet how much it will charge for access after that.
articles.author: By Boone Ashworth

articles.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
articles.description: CS 153 has gone viral on the Palo Alto campus—and on X. Not everyone is happy about it.
articles.author: By Maxwell Zeff

articles.title: Newly Deciphered Sabotage Malware May Have Targeted Iran’s Nuclear Program—and Predates Stuxnet
articles.description: Researchers have finally cracked Fast16, mysterious code capable of silently tampering with calculation and simulation software. It was created in 2005—and likely deployed by the US or an ally.
articles.author: By Andy Greenberg

articles.title: The WIRED Gear Team’s Tips on Ways to Save Money
articles.description: We pooled advice from our team—and a few financial experts—so you can worry less and save more in these turbulent times.
articles.author: By Julian Chokkattu

articles.title: One Way or Another, Most of Our Electricity Comes From Solar Power
articles.description: That’s good news, since the forecast is sunshine for the next 5 billion years.
articles.author: By Rhett Allain

articles.title: Palantir Employees Are Starting to Wonder if They're the Bad Guys
articles.description: Interviews with current and former Palantir employees, along with internal Slack messages obtained by WIRED, suggest a workforce in turmoil.
articles.author: By Makena Kelly

articles.title: MAGA Is Increasingly Convinced the Trump Assassination Attempt Was Staged
articles.description: Conspiracy theories about the Butler, Pennsylvania, shooting have ramped up in recent weeks as once steadfast Trump supporters turn on the president.
articles.author: By David Gilbert

articles.title: The 8 Best Movies to Stream This Month
articles.description: ’28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,’ ‘Crime 101,’ and ‘Deathstalker’ are among the films deserving of your eyeballs this month
articles.author: By Matt Kamen

articles.title: When a Houseplant Obsession Becomes a Nightmare
articles.description: Some of us just can’t resist the allure of the carnivorous Nepenthes. They’re beautiful, rare, and in every way life-consuming.
articles.author: By Brian Howey

articles.title: The Internet’s Favorite Lawyer Says We’re Living Through ‘Multiple Watergates per Week’
articles.description: Devin Stone turned punchy legal explainers into a YouTube empire. Now he’s warning that the sheer volume of Trump administration scandals is distorting reality itself.
articles.author: By Katie Drummond

articles.title: In the Wake of Anthropic’s Mythos, OpenAI Has a New Cybersecurity Model—and Strategy
articles.description: OpenAI says its safeguards “sufficiently reduce cyber risk” for now, while GPT-5.4-Cyber is a new cybersecurity-focused model.
articles.author: By Lily Hay Newman

articles.title: Scientists Have Made a French Fry Breakthrough
articles.description: Researchers have developed a method of making french fries that results in a healthier bite without sacrificing crispiness.
articles.author: By Marta Musso

articles.title: 5 Moon Mysteries That the Artemis Missions Could Finally Solve
articles.description: The moon is not just a barren rock orbiting the Earth. The Artemis missions could answer the great unknowns that the satellite holds.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: Where to Find the Energy to Save the World
articles.description: Jamie Beard is pouring everything into a singular vision: Tap into the awesome potential of geothermal power in Texas, and beyond. She has no time to lose.
articles.author: By Maria Streshinsky

articles.title: AI Drafting My Stories? Over My Dead Body
articles.description: AI-assisted writing is creeping into newsrooms under the guise of efficiency. But the trade-off may be more profound than publishers are willing to admit.
articles.author: By Steven Levy

articles.title: Spoofed Tankers Are Flooding the Strait of Hormuz. These Analysts Are Tracking Them
articles.description: Marine insurers and oil traders want to know what’s going on in one of the world’s most critical waterways. As the volume of disappearing ships in the area increases, analysts are getting creative.
articles.author: By Aarian Marshall

articles.title: The 10 Best TV Shows to Stream This Month
articles.description: ‘The Boys,’ ‘The Testaments,’ and ‘Stranger Things: Tales From ’85’ are just a few of the TV shows we’re bingeing this month.
articles.author: By Jennifer M. Wood

articles.title: The Men Behind Your Favorite AI Gay Thirst Traps
articles.description: A viral red carpet moment shone light on a group of hunky Instagram influencers—and the followers who are too horny to care that they’re not real.
articles.author: By Ej Dickson

articles.title: The US Military’s GPS Software Is an $8 Billion Mess
articles.description: The GPS Next-Generation Operational Control System was due for completion in 2016. Ten years later, the software for controlling the military’s GPS satellites still doesn’t work.
articles.author: By Stephen Clark, Ars Technica

articles.title: The Ghosts of Al-Shifa Hospital
articles.description: Months into a supposed ceasefire in Gaza, doctors still have to smuggle in basic medical supplies—and treat new casualties of war.
articles.author: By Spencer Ackerman

articles.title: No One Knows Where US Vaccine Policy Goes Next
articles.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s sweeping changes to federal vaccine guidance are paused for now. But even if they’re reversed, lasting damage has already been done.
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: The US Government Will Ask Data Centers How Much Power They Use
articles.description: In a letter obtained by WIRED, the Energy Information Administration tells two senators that it plans to develop a mandatory assessment of data centers' energy use.
articles.author: By Molly Taft

articles.title: Scientists Gave Cocaine to Salmon and You Will Absolutely Believe What Happened Next
articles.description: After scientists exposed wild fish to cocaine and a cocaine metabolite, they observed that, as in the lab, fish on cocaine do not act like normal fish.
articles.author: By Marta Musso

articles.title: Dark Matter May Be Made of Black Holes From Another Universe
articles.description: A model of the cyclic universe suggests that dark matter could be a population of black holes predating the Big Bang.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: Get Ready for a Year of Chaotic Weather in the US
articles.description: A massive Western heat wave and a potential El Niño event raise concerns about a long stretch of unpredictable and extreme weather.
articles.author: By Molly Taft

articles.title: Iran War Puts Global Energy Markets on the Brink of a Worst-Case Scenario
articles.description: “This will be so, so, so, so, so bad,” one analyst says.
articles.author: By Molly Taft

articles.title: The Weird, Twisting Tale of How China Spied on Alysa Liu and Her Dad
articles.description: Years before the figure skater became an Olympic superstar, a Chinese operative tried to stalk her father and monitored other US residents deemed dissidents against China. And that’s just the beginning.
articles.author: By Timothy McLaughlin

articles.title: The Pope’s Warnings About AI Were AI-Generated, a Detection Tool Claims
articles.description: Pangram Labs’ updated Chrome extension puts warning labels on AI slop as you scroll your social feeds.
articles.author: By Miles Klee

articles.title: The Gamblers Behind One of the Weirdest Cheating Mysteries in Chess Have Been Unmasked
articles.description: In July 1993, a disguised player entered the World Open chess tournament in Philadelphia using the name of a mathematician who died in 1957. His real identity remained unknown—until now.
articles.author: By Kit Chellel

articles.title: The Last Mystery of Antarctica’s ‘Blood Falls’ Has Finally Been Solved
articles.description: New research sheds light on what drives reddish water to emerge from underground to pour onto the Taylor Glacier.
articles.author: By Simone Valesini

articles.title: The AI Race Is Pressuring Utilities to Squeeze More From Europe’s Power Grids
articles.description: As data center developers queue up to connect to power grids across Europe, network operators are experimenting with novel ways of clearing room for them.
articles.author: By Joel Khalili

articles.title: The Caves That Could Help Us Find, or Become, Aliens
articles.description: From lava tubes on Mars to ice pockets on Europa, subterranean environments may offer the best chance of finding life—and living safely—beyond our planet.
articles.author: By Becky Ferreira

articles.title: 5 Reasons to Think Twice Before Using ChatGPT—or Any Chatbot—for Financial Advice
articles.description: As people increasingly rely on AI chatbots for guidance, even on financial matters, a healthy dose of skepticism is critical.
articles.author: By Reece Rogers

articles.title: Artemis II: Everything We Know as Its Crew Approaches the Far Side of the Moon
articles.description: Artemis II remains on course for its lunar flyby as the crew shares historic photos of Earth, tests key systems for future lunar missions, and attempts to fix the toilet.
articles.author: By Javier Carbajal

articles.title: The Adidas Hyperboost Edge Is Your New Super Trainer
articles.description: How do shoe companies translate superfast racing shoe tech into your daily trainers? We break it down with some of the best new running shoes on the market.
articles.author: By Kieran Alger

articles.title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
articles.description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.
articles.author: By Katie Drummond

articles.title: This Scammer Used an AI-Generated MAGA Girl to Grift ‘Super Dumb’ Men
articles.description: A med student says he’s made thousands of dollars selling photos and videos of a young conservative woman he created using generative tools. He’s not alone.
articles.author: By Ej Dickson

articles.title: This Beanie Is Designed to Read Your Thoughts
articles.description: California-based startup Sabi is developing a thought-to-text wearable that could usher in the cyborg future.
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: Flight Path Data Shows How Mosquitoes Target Humans
articles.description: Scientists have quantified what draws mosquitoes to people—which could help make better, life-saving bug traps.
articles.author: By Ritsuko Kawai

articles.title: Artemis II Mission Launches Successfully
articles.description: The crew of Artemis II will not descend to the moon, but their capsule will fly over the far side of its surface.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
articles.description: In this week’s episode of “Uncanny Valley,” we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of one of the world's biggest companies.
articles.author: By Brian Barrett

articles.title: What Happens When a Nuclear Site Is Hit?
articles.description: As strikes continue on Iran’s nuclear facilities, the real danger isn’t the explosion, but what happens if critical safety systems fail—and how that risk could spread across the Gulf.
articles.author: By Jethu Abraham

articles.title: Brain Gear Is the Hot New Wearable
articles.description: Smartwatches are cool and all, but have you considered wearable neurotech?
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: Artemis II Astronauts Return Safely to Earth After Historic Flight Around the Moon
articles.description: After traveling a greater distance from Earth than any humans before them, the astronauts of Artemis II have safely returned home.
articles.author: By Jay Bennett

articles.title: Artemis II’s Breathtaking View of the Far Side of the Moon
articles.description: The first images from Artemis II reveal what the moon looks like just 7,000 km from the surface—and confirm that NASA is ready to return to Earth’s satellite.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: How and When to Watch the Artemis II Mission’s Return to Earth
articles.description: Splashdown for the Artemis II crew is scheduled for Friday evening, and NASA will livestream the historic homecoming.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: You Should Be More Freaked Out by Shingles
articles.description: The viral infection leaves millions with chronic pain, increased stroke risk, and lifelong nerve damage—yet vaccination rates remain dangerously low.
articles.author: By Rosie Taylor

articles.title: Security News This Week: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU-s New Age-Verification App
articles.description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.
articles.author: By Dell Cameron

articles.title: The Shocking Secrets of Madison Square Garden’s Surveillance Machine
articles.description: Famously vengeful Knicks owner Jim Dolan has long spied on people at his iconic arenas. WIRED goes deep inside the operation that allegedly tracked a trans woman, lawyers, protesters, and more.
articles.author: By Noah Shachtman

articles.title: The Trip to the Far Side of the Moon
articles.description: As soon as April 1, four people will embark on a journey that will take them farther from the Earth than anyone has ever traveled before.
articles.author: By Jay Bennett

articles.title: Meet the Man Making Music With His Brain Implant
articles.description: Galen Buckwalter says brain-computer interfaces will have to be enjoyable to use if the technology is going to be successful.
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: 6,000 Meters Under the Pacific, Japan Seeks Independence From China on Rare Earths
articles.description: Tokyo is succeeding where the rest of the world has failed, reducing its reliance on Beijing for crucial rare earth elements—thanks to an enormous underwater deposit discovered on a remote island.
articles.author: By Lorenzo Lamperti

articles.title: How Can a Locomotive Pull a Long Train That’s Much Heavier?
articles.description: For railroads, it’s all about managing static and kinetic friction.
articles.author: By Rhett Allain

articles.title: Study of Buddhist Monks Finds Meditation Alters Brain Activity
articles.description: Meditation isn’t thinking about nothing. New research reinforces that it’s a mind-altering, dynamic state that promotes focus, learning, and well-being.
articles.author: By Javier Carbajal

articles.title: Artemis II Astronauts Witnessed 6 Meteorites Colliding With the Moon
articles.description: The moon gets hit by space debris all the time, but some of it is so large that the impact generates light that can be seen thousands of kilometers away.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: There’s New Evidence for How Loneliness Affects Memory in Old Age
articles.description: A longitudinal study found that loneliness is more closely linked to lapses in immediate and delayed recall than to the overall speed of cognitive decline.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: AI’s Next Frontier? An Algorithm for Consciousness
articles.description: Some of the world’s most interesting thinkers about thinking think they might’ve cracked machine sentience. And I think they might be onto something.
articles.author: By Will Knight

articles.title: Marine Animals in the Strait of Hormuz Don’t Get a Ceasefire
articles.description: As ships return to the Strait of Hormuz, mines, sonar, and congestion continue to reshape the Gulf beneath the surface.
articles.author: By Evangeline Elsa

articles.title: Rednote Draws a Line Between China and the World
articles.description: As the platform expands abroad, it’s taking steps to separate Chinese users from the international audiences it once brought together.
articles.author: By Zeyi Yang

articles.title: New York Bans Government Employees from Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
articles.description: A new executive order seen by WIRED prohibits New York state employees from using insider knowledge to enrich themselves with prediction market bets.
articles.author: By Kate Knibbs

articles.title: Mozilla Used Anthropic’s Mythos to Find and Fix 271 Bugs in Firefox
articles.description: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.
articles.author: By Lily Hay Newman

articles.title: Senators Demand to Know How Much Energy Data Centers Use
articles.description: In a letter sent Thursday morning, Elizabeth Warren and Josh Hawley press the Energy Information Agency to mandate annual electricity disclosure for data centers.
articles.author: By Molly Taft

articles.title: A New Study Details How Cats Almost Always Land on Their Feet
articles.description: The secret to this acrobatic skill lies in an extremely flexible part of the spine that allows cats to twist in the air and land safely.
articles.author: By Marta Musso

articles.title: Scientists Gave Cocaine to Salmon and You Will Absolutely Believe What Happened Next
articles.description: After scientists exposed wild fish to cocaine and a cocaine metabolite, they observed that, as in the lab, fish on cocaine do not act like normal fish.
articles.author: By Marta Musso

articles.title: War Memes Are Turning Conflict Into Content
articles.description: The jokes were funny. The systems behind them—and the reasons we keep passing around war memes as entertainment—are more serious.
articles.author: By Rand Al-Hadethi

articles.title: 5 AI Models Tried to Scam Me. Some of Them Were Scary Good
articles.description: The cyber capabilities of AI models have experts rattled. AI’s social skills may be just as dangerous.
articles.author: By Will Knight

articles.title: Sam Altman’s Orb Company Promoted a Bruno Mars Partnership That Doesn't Exist
articles.description: “To be clear, we were never approached … nor were we in any discussions regarding a partnership or tour access,” a spokesperson for the artist tells WIRED.
articles.author: By Maxwell Zeff

articles.title: USAID Whistleblower Says It Was Even Worse Than People Knew
articles.description: Political appointees wanted a quiet drawdown, the whistleblower says. DOGE wanted an execution.
articles.author: By Vittoria Elliott

articles.title: The Best Robot Vacuums
articles.description: Tired of vacuuming? Give the reins to a robot vacuum. Here are the best robot vacuums I tried so far this year.
articles.author: By Nena Farrell

articles.title: A Quantum Leap for the Turing Award
articles.description: Charles Bennett and Gilles Brassard pioneered quantum information theory. Now they've been awarded the highest honor in computer science.
articles.author: By Steven Levy

articles.title: Security News This Week: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
articles.description: Plus: Iran’s internet blackout hits the 1,000-hour mark, cryptocurrency scams result in a record amount of money stolen from Americans, and more.
articles.author: By Matt Burgess

articles.title: These Are the 4 Artemis II Astronauts Leading the Historic Return to the Moon
articles.description: The Artemis II mission crew includes the first woman, the first Black person, and the first non-American astronaut to travel to the lunar environment.
articles.author: By Fernanda González

articles.title: The Best Fitbit Depends on Your Goals. Here's How to Choose
articles.description: Ready to start or reignite your fitness routine? I've rounded up the best Fitbit models for every stage of your journey.
articles.author: By Boutayna Chokrane

articles.title: How the Vision Pro Rollout Inflamed Tensions at Apple
articles.description: Even before the headset’s release, the workforce at Apple Stores was under duress. Trying to get customers interested in the Vision Pro made it worse.
articles.author: By Noam Scheiber

articles.title: You Can Approximate Pi by Dropping Needles on the Floor
articles.description: Who needs a supercomputer when you can calculate pi with a box of sewing needles?
articles.author: By Rhett Allain

articles.title: FDA Approves Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 Pill
articles.description: Eli Lilly’s once-daily Foundayo is the second obesity pill to receive FDA approval. It will compete with Novo Nordisk’s pill version of Wegovy.
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: The Future of the Artemis Program Is Riding on Reentry
articles.description: Entire space programs have been canceled after a failure in the reentry phase. In the final test for Artemis II, astronauts will travel at 32 times the speed of sound as they return from the moon.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: USAID Whistleblower Says It Was Even Worse Than People Knew
articles.description: Political appointees wanted a quiet drawdown, the whistleblower says. DOGE wanted an execution.
articles.author: By Vittoria Elliott

articles.title: Uncanny Valley: OpenAI and Musk Fight Again; DOJ Mishandles Voter Data; Artemis II Comes Home
articles.description: In this episode, the hosts discuss the fight between OpenAI and Elon Musk, the misuse of voter data, and Artemis II’s moonshot.
articles.author: By Brian Barrett

articles.title: How Can Astronauts Tell How Fast They’re Going?
articles.description: Weirdly, spaceships have no direct way to gauge their own speed. Luckily, we can use some physics tricks to figure it out.
articles.author: By Rhett Allain

articles.title: There’s a Secret Ingredient to Making Luxury Ice at Home
articles.description: Nice ice is big business, but you can get perfectly clear cubes at home without freezing your assets.
articles.author: By Jeremy White

articles.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
articles.description: From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.
articles.author: By Gia Chaudry

articles.title: How to Watch the Lyrids Meteor Shower at Its Peak
articles.description: Between the night of April 21 and the early morning of April 22, those looking in the right place will see the sky light up with 15 to 20 meteors per hour.
articles.author: By Jorge Garay

articles.title: ‘Beer, Titties, Freedom’: Inside the World’s Toughest Off-Road Race
articles.description: Every winter, 80,000 people gather in the California desert for King of the Hammers, a popular racing series featuring high-speed relays and rock crawling with dirt bikes, stock VW Bugs, and million-dollar trophy trucks.
articles.author: By Matthew Reamer

articles.title: US Senate Candidate Caught Insider Trading on Kalshi Says He Did It on Purpose
articles.description: Mark Moran, an underdog Senate candidate from Virginia, claims he wanted to get caught violating the prediction market platform’s rules.
articles.author: By Kate Knibbs

articles.title: A Startup Says It Grew Human Sperm in a Lab—and Used It to Make Embryos
articles.description: Paterna Biosciences says it has determined the set of instructions needed to turn sperm-making stem cells into "normal, mature" sperm.
articles.author: By Emily Mullin

articles.title: Pan Am Flight 103: Robert Mueller’s 30-Year Search for Justice
articles.description: Known as the Lockerbie bombing, in December 1988 a bomb downed a Pan Am jet, leaving 270 dead. It was the first mass killing of Americans by terrorists. As the head of the Justice Department’s criminal division, Robert Mueller oversaw the case. And for him, it was personal.
articles.author: By Garrett M. Graff
